folio.label: 111v
folio.l: And mad al seker I now , witú holynes

note.notetxt: 464The ink here becomes again lighter brown. Also note that this line is written above the ruling lineODR

folio.l: THus ben they wedded , with solempnyte
And at the fest sittetú , he and she
Witú othere worthy folk , vp oon the deys
Al ful of ioye and blisse , is the paleys
And ful of Instrumentez , and of vetaille
The most deynteuoë , of al Ytaille
Byforn~ hem were there , Instrumentes of swicú soun
That Orpheus , nor Thebes Amphioun
Ne maden neuäe , swicú a melody
At euäy cours , than cam loude Mynstralsy
That neuäe tromped , Ioab , for to here
Ne he Theodomas , half so clere
At Thebes , whan the Citee was in doute
Bacus , the wyn , hem shynketú al a boute
And venus laugúetú , vp oon euäy wygút
For Ianuarie , was bycome hire knygút
And wolde bothe assayen , his corage
In liberte , and eke in mariage
And witú hire fyrbrond , in hire hand a boute
Dauncetú byforn~ the Bryde , and al the route
And certeynly , I dar wel seyn this
That Ymeneus , whicú god of weddyngö is
Saw neuäe his lyf , so merye a wedded man
Holde æè thy pees , thow Poete Marcian
That wrytest vs , that ilke weddyngö mery
Of hire Philosophie , and hym Marcury
And of the songes , that the Muses songö
To smal is bothe thy penne , and eke thy tongö
For to discryuen , of this mariage
Whan tendre ©outhe , hatú wedded stoupyngö age
There is swicú myrthe , that it may nat be wretyn
Assayetú it ©oure self , than may ©e wetyn
If that I lye , or noon , in this matere
Mayus that syt , witú so benigne a chere
Hire to byholde , it semed feyrie
Quene Ester , loked neuäe witú swicú an eye
On assure , so meke a loke hatú she
I may ©ow nat deuyse , al hire beaute
But thus meche , of hire beaute , tellen I may
That she was lyke , the brigút morwe of May
Fulfilled of al beaute , and plesaunce
This Ianuarie , is rauysshed , in a traunce
At euäy tyme , he loked oon hire face
But in his hert , he gan hire to manace
That he that nygút , in armes wolde hire streyne
